content.problemStatement:
Ryan is the president of a planet. There is a large bus station on this planet and MMM buses stop at this station every day. For each valid iii, the iii-th bus arrives at the station tit_iti‚Äč seconds after midnight (on Ryan's planet, each day lasts 1010010^{100}10100 hours, not 242424 hours), waits at the station for some non-negative (not necessarily integer) amount of time and then leaves.

Ryan knows that NNN people are coming to the station today. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the iii-th person is described by parameters sis_isi‚Äč, aia_iai‚Äč, bib_ibi‚Äč, cic_ici‚Äč, did_idi‚Äč with the following meaning:


This person arrives at the station sis_isi‚Äč seconds after midnight with dissatisfaction 000.
If there is at least one bus that leaves the station strictly less than aia_iai‚Äč seconds before this person arrives, their dissatisfaction increases by bib_ibi‚Äč.
If this person has to wait at the station for at least cic_ici‚Äč seconds before boarding a bus and leaving (this includes the time they spend waiting for a bus and the time spent waiting for that bus to leave), their dissatisfaction increases by did_idi‚Äč.
Ryan may be the president, but his power is quite limited. He can tell each bus driver when to leave the station and each passenger when to board a bus (these times do not have to be integers). He also has to follow one rule: it is not allowed for two buses to be at the station at the same time.

As the president, his job is to make the citizens happy. Therefore, he is wondering about the minimum total dissatisfaction (the sum of final dissatisfactions of all NNN passengers) he can achieve by using his power optimally. Of course, as an assistant to the president, you are the one who has to calculate that for him.

Note that each passenger should board a bus at some point (during the day when they arrive at the station). Ryan does not want to leave someone hanging there for an eternity. Also, a passenger is allowed to board a bus at the moment when that bus is leaving (even if that passenger just arrived at the station at that moment).
constraints: 1‚Č§si,ai,bi,ci,di‚Č§1091 \le s_i, a_i, b_i, c_i, d_i \le 10^91‚Č§si‚Äč,ai‚Äč,bi‚Äč,ci‚Äč,di‚Äč‚Č§109 for each valid iii
t1,t2,‚Ä¶,tM,s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_M, s_1, s_2, \ldots, s_Nt1‚Äč,t2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,tM‚Äč,s1‚Äč,s2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äč are pairwise distinct
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer MMM.
The second line contains MMM space-separated integers t1,t2,‚Ä¶,tMt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_Mt1‚Äč,t2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,tM‚Äč.
The third line contains a single integer NNN.
The fourth line contains NNN space-separated integers s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sNs_1, s_2, \ldots, s_Ns1‚Äč,s2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,sN‚Äč.
The fifth line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
The sixth line contains NNN space-separated integers b1,b2,‚Ä¶,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,bN‚Äč.
The seventh line contains NNN space-separated integers c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äč,c2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äč.
The eighth line contains NNN space-separated integers d1,d2,‚Ä¶,dNd_1, d_2, \ldots, d_Nd1‚Äč,d2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,dN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the minimum total dissatisfaction of the passengers.
